mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth signals labor market strength; a sustained positive trend in PAYEMS indicates rising aggregate demand and earnings, which boosts corporate profits and drives S&P 500 positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between PAYEMS growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is zero or negative at any point in the backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate falling below 4.5% signals a tight labor market that forces the Fed to raise rates, tightening financial conditions and compressing equity valuations; this yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of trigger instances, or average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising Federal Funds rate simultaneously with declining housing starts indicates monetary tightening is actively cooling the housing market, an early warning of economic slowdown that leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS change > 0 and HOUST change < 0 over 3 months
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative forward returns in less than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction coefficient in logistic regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation remains below 2.5% year-over-year, the economy operates without overheating pressure; the Fed can keep rates accommodative, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered periods, or median return is not significantly above zero (signed-rank p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing sector output, which drives corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-trading-day horizon as the market prices in real economic strength.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth has zero or negative correlation with S&P 500 5-day forward returns over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth above 0.75% signals robust aggregate demand, lifting profit expectations and driving S&P 500 positive returns over the subsequent 60 trading days; weak growth would have the opposite effect.
failure_condition: Average 60-day return following strong GDP releases is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling unemployment signals that consumer spending is accelerating while labor market slack diminishes — a powerful pro-cyclical impulse that lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS positive change and UNRATE negative change over 1 month
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive forward returns in less than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term in a regression model is not significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is above 5%, tight monetary policy restricts economic activity through higher borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return during high-rate periods is not statistically different from zero or is positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
